label: very_low
derivation: v3 derivation, priors for calibrated review, not an empirical claim. Register loss-event-frequency convention (recalibrated 2026-07-19, owner-approved: under the D3 encoding a register likelihood IS the loss-event frequency, so bands anchor on once-in-X-years matrix semantics, up to ~1 decade below the earlier domain-neutral log-decade scale for the upper bands (very_low unchanged)): very_low = 0.01-0.1 events/yr (once in 10-100 years). Mode = geometric midpoint sqrt(low*high) of the band, rounded to 2 significant figures. O-RA 2.0.1 publishes NO frequency example scale (S5.2 gives estimation guidance only) - this scale is a v3 convention, not O-RA-cited.

label: very_low
derivation: O-RA Table 1 (§6.6, p.33 — "An Example Scale Translating Quantitative Values to Qualitative Labels") anchors this band: Very Low (VL) < $10K. Two honest caveats recorded here: (a) O-RA presents Table 1 as an example scale that "should be guided by scales that have been approved by management" — the org override layer implements exactly that approval step; (b) O-RA's direction of use is output-translation (quantitative → qualitative); this canonical table repurposes the same edges input-ward as priors, which is exactly the move §6.5 (pp.32-33) cautions against — mitigated structurally by DRAFT status and never-auto-final review, not by this table alone. The $1K floor closes O-RA's open lower end; a documented v3 choice, not O-RA-cited. Mode = geometric midpoint √(low×high) of the closed band, rounded to 2 significant figures — a v3 derivation choice, not from O-RA Table 1 (which supplies band edges only).

label: very_high
derivation: O-RA Table 1 (§6.6, p.33 — "An Example Scale Translating Quantitative Values to Qualitative Labels") anchors this band: Severe (SV) > $10M. Two honest caveats recorded here: (a) O-RA presents Table 1 as an example scale that "should be guided by scales that have been approved by management" — the org override layer implements exactly that approval step; (b) O-RA's direction of use is output-translation (quantitative → qualitative); this canonical table repurposes the same edges input-ward as priors, which is exactly the move §6.5 (pp.32-33) cautions against — mitigated structurally by DRAFT status and never-auto-final review, not by this table alone. The $1B cap ≈ p99.9 of the scenario library's σ≈2.27 catastrophic-loss cluster, ~1 order of magnitude below the heaviest supply-chain tails' p99.9 (≈$20B, σ=3.47) — the catastrophic class itself stays deliberately unbounded; this cap is a documented v3 choice closing O-RA's open upper end, not O-RA-cited. Mode = geometric midpoint √(low×high) of the closed band, rounded to 2 significant figures — a v3 derivation choice, not from O-RA Table 1 (which supplies band edges only).
